description_long: This object consists of two large, roughly cuboid stone blocks with a marbled, tan-to-light-brown coloration and subtle variations in hue. The surfaces are irregular and slightly chipped, with some visible textural roughness and natural imperfections typical of quarried stone. The blocks are positioned at an angle to each other, suggesting they may have been stacked or shifted from their original alignment. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. The coloration and texture indicate a sedimentary or limestone material, commonly used in urban landscaping or as barriers. There are no visible engravings, markings, or artificial modifications on the surfaces.
description: Two large, marbled tan stone blocks with rough, chipped surfaces and natural textural variations. The blocks are solid, dense, and slightly misaligned.
description_view_0: Front view showing both blocks, with the larger block in the background and the smaller block slightly rotated in the foreground.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angular misalignment and the marbled texture on the largest visible face.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the larger block's broad face, with less of the smaller block visible.
description_view_3: Oblique view emphasizing the rough, chipped edges and the color variation across both blocks.
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set on compacted ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: Minor chipping and surface roughness, with some color variation suggesting exposure to the elements. No significant moss or erosion visible.
